title: palicourea subalata
intro: Palicourea subalata is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the red ironbark. It is native to Australia and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its health benefits, which include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Palicourea subalata can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, for its medicinal and therapeutic uses.
